latest.title: Disposal of Atlanta fulfilment centre
latest.content:
1 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4790K
ASOS PLC
01 July 2026
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION
1 July 2026
ASOS Plc
Global Online Fashion Destination
"ASOS" or the "Group"
Disposal of Atlanta fulfilment centre for c.£48m;marks completion of non-core asset sale programme
ASOS today announces that it has signed and completed the assignment of the lease of its Atlanta fulfilment centre and the disposal of the associated automation assets (together, the "Transaction"). The lease has been assigned to a global consumer brand and the assets are being purchased by a member of the DHL Group. The Transaction marks a further strengthening of the Group's financial position, with net proceeds of c.£48m (c.$63m) and annual cash cost savings of c.£6m at current exchange rates (c.$8m).
Strategic Rationale: Continued execution of ASOS's Efficient Operating Model
The disposal of the Atlanta fulfilment centre represents a further step in ASOS's programme to strengthen its balance sheet and deliver disciplined capital allocation. The site is non-operational and was fully written down in prior periods, and the Transaction enables the Group to realise value from a non-core asset while continuing to simplify its asset base and improve cash generation.
This disposal builds on a series of actions to improve the Group's financial position, including the refinancing in November 2025, repayment of the 2026 convertible bonds in April 2026 and the disposal of the Lichfield fulfilment centre in May 2026. Net proceeds will be retained to support financial flexibility as ASOS continues to execute its strategy.
Financial Impact
The Transaction has resulted in net sale proceeds of c.£48m, with annual cash savings of c.£6m relating to rent and other occupancy costs. There is also a one-off profit before tax of c.£78m at current exchange rates (c.$103m) after adjustments to associated property liabilities which will be recognised as an adjusting item in the Group's financial results for FY26.
The net proceeds will add to the Group's cash position of £209.5m as at 1 March 2026. Taking into account the c.£67m net proceeds from the disposal of the Lichfield fulfilment centre and the c.£48m net proceeds from the Atlanta Transaction, the pro forma net debt (excluding lease liabilities) position is c.£180m (net debt was c.£295m as at 1 March 2026).
Jose Antonio Ramos, ASOS CEO, said:
"The disposal of Atlanta is another clear demonstration of us delivering on our commitments - strengthening the balance sheet, simplifying the business and maintaining strict discipline in how we allocate capital. Since closing the Atlanta fulfilment centre, we have successfully transitioned to our new US operating model, giving customers access to a broader product assortment from our global inventory. The strength of customer engagement gives us confidence in this approach and reinforces the progress we are making as we execute our strategy and reposition ASOS for long-term, sustainable growth."
The person responsible for arranging the release of this announcement on behalf of the Company is Rishi Sharma, General Counsel and Company Secretary.
For further information:
ASOS plc
Phil Clark, Head of Strategy & Investor Relations
Email: InvestorEnquiries@asos.com
Hannah Alderman, Investor Relations Manager
Teneo
Jonathan Sibun / Will Palfreyman
Tel: 020 7353 4200
About ASOS
Founded in 2000, ASOS has 17m active customers in over 100 markets. We bring fashion lovers around the world the best and most relevant fashion through our unique own brands including ASOS DESIGN, ARRANGE, COLLUSION, Topshop and Topman, styled with the most exciting products from local and global partner brands. With our expert in-house design team and agile and flexible commercial model, including ASOS Fulfilment Services, Partner Fulfils, and Test & React, we make the latest trends accessible to all and give customers the confidence to be whoever they want to be.

latest.title: Director Notification
latest.content:
9 Jun 2026 16:34
RNS Number : 6361H
ASOS PLC
09 June 2026
9 June 2026
ASOS Plc
("ASOS" or the "Company")
Director Notification
Christine Cross, Independent Non-Executive Director, has informed the Board of her intention not to seek re-election at the next Annual General Meeting ("AGM") of the Company, which is expected to be held in early 2027, in order to focus on other personal and professional commitments.
Christine has served as Chair of the Remuneration Committee and as a member of the Audit Committee since her appointment in April 2024. ASOS is following its succession planning process to recruit an Independent Non-Executive Director to Chair the Remuneration Committee and the Company will announce the successor in due course.
Natasja Laheij, ASOS Chair, said:
"On behalf of the Board, I would like to thank Christine for her significant contribution to the Company during her tenure. She has played an important role in re-shaping the Company's Executive Remuneration and has been a valued member of the Board. We respect Christine's decision not to seek re-election and wish her all the best for the future".
Christine Cross, Independent Non-Executive Director, said:
"It has been a privilege to serve on the Board of ASOS. I am proud of what we have achieved during my time with the Company and wish the Board and management continued success".
For further information:
ASOS plc
Alex Dent, Deputy Company Secretary
Tel: 020 7756 1000
Teneo
Jonathan Sibun / Will Palfreyman
Tel: 020 7353 4200

Founded in 2000, ASOS has 17m active customers in over 150 markets. We bring fashion lovers around the world the best and most relevant fashion through our unique own brands including ASOS DESIGN, ARRANGE, COLLUSION, Topshop, and Topman, styled with the most exciting products from local and global partner brands. With our expert in-house design team and agile and flexible commercial model, including ASOS Fulfilment Services, Partner Fulfils, and Test & React, we make the latest trends accessible to all and give customers the confidence to be whoever they want to be.

latest.title: Lichfield Fulfilment Centre Disposal Completion
latest.content:
28 May 2026 16:23
RNS Number : 1176G
ASOS PLC
28 May 2026
28 May 2026
ASOS Plc
Global Online Fashion Destination
("ASOS" or the "Company")
ASOS completes the disposal of Lichfield fulfilment centre
ASOS is pleased to announce that, further to its announcement on 11 May 2026, it has today completed the disposal of the Lichfield Fulfilment Centre to Marks & Spencer Plc (the
"Transaction"
).
There has been no material change affecting any matter contained in the announcement relating to the Transaction released by the Company on 11 May 2026. As previously stated, the total cash consideration is £67.5m with net sales proceeds of at least £66m and is expected to result in a one-off profit before tax of c.£85m after adjustments to associated property liabilities. The proceeds have been received on completion, with all conditions precent to completion satisfied, and will be reflected in the Company's FY26 financial statements.
For more information regarding the Transaction including background, rationale and use of proceeds, please see the announcement released by the Company on 11 May 2026.
For further information:
ASOS plc

company_30s.what_they_do: ASC.L runs a global e‑commerce fulfilment network. It owns and operates warehouses that pick, pack and ship products for online retailers, offering end‑to‑end logistics solutions from storage to last‑mile delivery.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the heart of the rapidly expanding online retail sector. With the shift to digital shopping, efficient fulfilment is a critical bottleneck. By controlling key distribution hubs, ASC.L can command higher margins and lock in long‑term contracts with major retailers.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has recently sold its Atlanta fulfilment centre to refocus on its core UK and European operations. Cash flow is tight, but the company is actively raising capital and restructuring its balance sheet. The share price sits well below historic levels, reflecting a steep sell‑off that many market participants have ignored.

rns_narrative: The company is trimming its US footprint, selling the Atlanta centre to free up capital and streamline operations in its main markets.

Governance moves are in play – new director filings, shareholding disclosures, and a block listing application suggest the board is preparing for a potential listing change or significant capital raise.
Financial updates are coming: interim results, AGM outcomes, and a refinancing announcement indicate that the firm is tightening its financial base and seeking better debt terms.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet skepticism turning into cautious optimism – traders are waiting for a clear catalyst before committing.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Nate_D and a handful of others point to a technical breakout from 340.5 support toward 375.5, implying a potential upside if the price breaks out of the current range.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: DV2026 and some bearish tweets warn that political uncertainty around business rate cuts and increased share ownership could stall any rebound, leaving the stock exposed to further downside.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter is shifting from flat to a split view: a small bullish clique sees a breakout, while the majority remains wary of political and liquidity risks.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – there is no public buzz, meaning any news or catalyst could catch the market off‑guard.

near_term_catalysts: The July AGM on 21 Jul 2026 will release updated earnings and a debt‑restructuring plan – a clear event that could spark a bounce.
Any announcement of new fulfilment contracts or a refinancing deal would improve cash flow and investor sentiment.
A volume surge that pushes the price back above the 340.5 support level would signal strong institutional buying.
expect_next.watch_for: Keep an eye on daily volume – a jump to >1 million shares traded and a close above 340.5 would confirm the bounce thesis. Also watch for a price break to 375.5 as a further confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price appears to be in the early stage of a deep crash bottom (well below 70% of its peak). The APEX score suggests tight compression, so a mean‑reversion move is likely once a catalyst appears.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down more than 70% from its recent high, creating a huge upside if it recovers to the 340.5 support level and then to 375.5. Historically, deep crashes have delivered 39%+ returns, and with a 70% drop the potential gain is roughly 1.5× the current price – a 150% upside if the bounce reaches 375.5.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Political risk around business rate cuts and the company’s increasing share ownership could dampen demand for its services. If the AGM fails to deliver a clear debt plan, the stock could keep sliding into a new low, with a realistic downside of 30–40% from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: a 20% drop from the current price (if it slides further into a new low). Reward: a 150% gain if the price climbs to 375.5. The asymmetry is roughly 1:7 – a small loss for a large potential gain.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce back to 340.5 support, ideally after a volume spike. Set a stop 5% below that level (around 25p). Allocate 15–20% of your trading capital to this position, scaling in if the price confirms the breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the AGM on 21 Jul 2026. If the company announces a refinancing or a new contract, look for a volume surge and a price break above 340.5. That would be a clear entry trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has been identified; therefore, if the price fails to recover by early Q4 2026, consider tightening the stop or exiting to protect capital.

ai_insights.bottom_line: ASC.L sits at a deep crash bottom with no public buzz – a perfect contrarian setup that could rebound dramatically once the AGM delivers a clear debt plan, offering a 150% upside for a modest downside risk.
